modmenu.descriptionTranslation.pishock-z: Connect PiShock devices to Minecraft! Like hardcore mode, but with real consequences. Zap yourself when you take damage or die!
modmenu.summaryTranslation.pishock-zap: Zap yourself with PiShock when you take damage.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.general.vibra:
Only vibrate the device, don't shock, even on death.
This can be used to test the mod or simply
provide a less intense experience.
For example, if you want to check your share codes work.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.general.shock:
Shock the user on death with a
configurable intensity and duration.
If disabled, normal damage shocks will still occur.

Shock the user with an intensity
based on remaining health instead of damage.
This may encourage the user to stay healed up.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.general.fract:
Calculate on exact damage values,
even if it is not visible in the health bar.
This can make things more interesting when you are
heavily armored, or have resistance effects.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.durati: The duration of the shock/vibration by default
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.max_du:
The maximum duration of the shock/vibration
if the duration is accumulated
(See backoff settings)
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.vibrat:
How hard you have to get damaged to get shocked.
Anything below this will give a vibration.
Set to 0% to disable vibration, or
to 100% to save shocks for death only.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.min_da:
The amount of damage which will result in
the minimum intensity of shocks/vibrations.
Anything below this will be the minimum intensity.
This is 5% (usually half a heart) by default,
but can be changed if you have non-standard
max hp, or if you want to experience
lower intensities more often.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.max_da:
The amount of damage which will result in
the maximum intensity of shocks/vibrations.
Anything above this will be the maximum intensity.
This is 100% by default, but can be decreased
if you want to experience higher intensities
more often.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.vibrat:
The minimum intensity of the vibration
on damage

The maximum intensity of the vibration
on damage (when just at threshold)
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.shock_:
The minimum intensity of shocks on damage
(when just above threshold)

The maximum intensity of shocks on damage
i.e. when the player takes the enough damage
to reach the "max shock intensity" threshold,
or the highest it can accumulate to via backoff

The intensity of shocks on death.
This can be above your usual maximum.

The duration of shocks on death
This can be above your usual maximum.
Which devices to activate on damage
Which devices to activate on death
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.debounce.accu:
Accumulate the duration of shocks/vibrations
If this is enabled, queued shocks/vibrations will be
combined and their durations added together.
Limits are of course still respected.
If weaker shocks are queued, they will
add less to the duration.

Accumulate the intensity of shocks/vibrations
If this is enabled, queued shocks/vibrations will be
combined and their intensities added together.
Limits are of course still respected.
This is similar to the effect of taking the damage all at once.
If this is disabled, the intensity of the resulting shock/vibration
will be the greatest of the queued shocks/vibrations.
Disabling it is easier on the user :)
If shock-on-health is enabled, this will be ignored.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.debounce.queu:
Queue differently-sized shocks/vibrations separately
If this is enabled, during backoff, differently-sized
shocks/vibrations will not accumulate duration with
each other, and will instead be sent separately.
Disables intensity accumulation.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api_type: The type of API to use for PiShock communication
description.pishock-zap.config.api_type: It is recommended to use the %1$s API unless you have a specific reason to use another API type.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.test_conn: Silently test connection to this API
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.test_vibr: Send a vibrate command to this API
description.pishock-zap.config.api.local: PiShock Local Serial API is intended for advanced users only. It requires the PiShock device to be directly connected via USB to the computer running Minecraft. The advantage is that it provides near-instant feedback unlike the Web API which is constrained by network latency.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.serial_po:
The path or name of the serial port to use
for local serial API communication
For example, COM3 or /dev/ttyUSB0
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.device_id: The IDs of the shockers to drive via local serial API
description.pishock-zap.config.api.local: Shocker IDs are short numeric identifiers available from the %1$s. You can find them by clicking on the settings icon in the bottom-center of each individual controller. This is not the same as your PiShock device ID.
error.pishock-zap.config.api.local.devic: Need at least one device ID
Device IDs must be a positive number
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.local.add:
Request shocker device IDs from
the connected PiShock
description.pishock-zap.config.api.webho:
Advanced users only. This calls out to a webhook URL with a JSON payload similar to the below:

%1$s

This allows you to integrate with your own custom systems.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.webhook.p:
The operation to perform.
One of: %1$s

The intensity of the operation.
An integer between 0 and 100

The duration of the operation.
A number in seconds

The shock distribution of the operation.
One of: %1$s
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.custom_we: The URL of the webhook to send data to
description.pishock-zap.config.api.opens: This mod contacts the OpenShock servers, and makes requests to them over the internet when a shock or vibrate operation is requested. This data is only sent to the OpenShock API and is not shared with any other service.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.openshock: The IDs of the shockers to use (not share codes!)

Use your API token to automatically
fetch device IDs from the server
description.pishock-zap.config.api.opens: OpenShock Local Serial API is intended for advanced users only. It requires the OpenShock device to be directly connected via USB to the computer running Minecraft. In exchange, it provides near-instant response times and high configurability.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.openshock: Shock collar model and pairing IDs
The model of the shock collar

Use your API key to fetch all shock devices
listed on your OpenShock account
description.pishock-zap.config.api.pisho: This mod contacts the PiShock servers, and makes requests to them over the internet when a shock or vibrate operation is requested. This data is only sent to the PiShock API and is not shared with any other service.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.pishock.w: PiShock hubs and their associated shockers

The numeric user ID of your PiShock account.
I recommend you fetch this automatically.
error.pishock-zap.config.api.pishock.web: User ID must be a positive number
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.pishock.w:
Automatically fetch user ID, hub IDs, and shocker IDs.
Requires API key and username to be set.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.logging.l:
What this mod should identify itself as in the logs.
This usually shows up in the web interface,
so you can tell which shocks came from this mod.
description.pishock-zap.config.api.pisho: You can find your username and API key on the %1$s. Share codes are available from the %2$s (click the "share" button to generate a share code).
error.pishock-zap.config.api.share_codes: Need at least one share code
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.openshock: An API token with access to your shockers
tooltip.pishock-zap.open_config: Click to open the PiShock-Zap configuration screen
